label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage and see which question has not been discussed. First, the passage says that men have always believed they are smarter than women. Then a study shows that men are more in the top 2% and also more in the bottom 2%. The study's participants were tested on various subjects. The aggregate scores of men and women were similar. One of the authors, Professor Timothy Bates, suggests that men have been expected to be high achievers while women are restricted to home duties. He mentions the female developmental program may be tilted towards ensuring survival and the middle ground. The study aligns with past results where men are more likely to get first or third class degrees, while women get seconds. It also says men take more risks in academics while women are steadier. A past study showed women get more firsts and seconds, while men get more thirds. The argument is that coursework (which favors women's steady approach) is increasing over exams. Now, looking at the options: (A) Why are men smarter than women? -
